title: Delphinium subtubulosum
taxonomy: Delphinium subtubulosum is classified within the kingdom Plantae, specifically within the family Ranunculaceae, commonly known as the buttercup or crowfoot family. This classification is based on the plant's characteristics and relationships to other organisms. Within the family Ranunculaceae, Delphinium subtubulosum belongs to the genus Delphinium, which consists of a group of flowering plants known as delphiniums or larkspurs. The genus Delphinium is further divided into several species, with Delphinium subtubulosum being a distinct species within this group. This classification reflects the plant's evolutionary history and its position within the plant kingdom.

side_effects: Delphinium subtubulosum, like other plants in the Delphinium genus, may cause gastrointestinal issues, such as nausea and vomiting, due to its toxic compounds. Ingesting parts of the plant can also lead to cardiac and nervous system problems. Skin contact with the plant may cause irritation and allergic reactions in some individuals. intro: Delphinium subtubulosum, also known as the subterranean larkspur, is a perennial plant species belonging to the Ranunculaceae family, characterized by its tall spikes of blue to purple flowers. The plant has been traditionally used in herbal medicine for its potential health benefits, including anti-inflammatory and antispasmodic properties, which may help alleviate symptoms of anxiety, insomnia, and gastrointestinal issues. The bioactive constituents of Delphinium subtubulosum include alkaloids, such as delphinine and ajacine, which are responsible for its medicinal effects. The most common herbal preparations made with Delphinium subtubulosum include teas, tinctures, and infusions, although its use is often limited due to the risk of toxicity associated with its alkaloid content.
